title: Which portable power station is right for you? Bluetti’s battery guide breaks it down
summary: Power outages caused by severe storms can often last for hours, or days – and extreme heat just compounds the issue, making it harder to keep food and medicine safe when you need it. With summer heatwaves and hurricane season arriving at the same time, the question for many households isn’t if they should prepare for outages, it’s how.

title: America’s most affordable new car is THIS electric pickup, but Ford is catching up
summary: On today’s officially priced episode of Quick Charge, Jeff Bezos-backed Slate Auto has announced official pricing for its compact, ultra-simple electric pickup and SUV – and this compact truck just so happens to be the most affordable new car you can buy in the US!

title: Kia will present three new PV5 variants at BIMOS this week, including a 7-seater
summary: The PV5, Kia’s electric van, comes in many “top hats” for everyday and business use. At the Busan International Mobility Show that opens on June 26, Kia will introduce three new derivatives, including a 7-seater.

title: Tesla, Sunrun team up on 16 GW virtual power plant for data centers
summary:
Sunrun, Tesla, and Renew Home announced an agreement today to aggregate more than 16 gigawatts of home batteries, thermostats, and other devices into what they say would be the largest distributed power plant in the country, aimed squarely at the surging electricity demand from data centers.

The deal sent Sunrun (RUN) shares up as much as 26% on Wednesday, as Wall Street bet the residential solar company can turn AI’s power crunch into a recurring revenue stream.

title: Hyundai and Kia test the ‘world’s first’ in-car sanitization tech in the PV5
summary: Hyundai and Kia have a new idea to keep your interior germ-free. The Korean automakers revealed “the world’s first in-vehicle sanitization technology,” after a successful test in the PV5.

title: Prime Day-2 Deals: Bluetti Prime Day Sale with exclusive new power station lows, Segway Navimow X4 robot mower lows, more
summary:
It’s the second day of Amazon’s Prime Day savings event, and we’ve got another jam-packed edition of Green Deals for you – plus, all the other major savings during this week that’s been collected into our regularly updated Prime Day Green Deals roundup hub here. Our three headliners are all seeing tons of new low prices, starting with Bluetti’s increased Prime Day Sale savings on power stations, like the 3,014Wh Elite 300 Portable Power Station at a new and exclusive $949 low. Right behind it is Segway Navimow’s Prime Day event that is dropping the premium X430 + X450 Robotic Lawn Mowers to new lows starting from $2,099, among legacy models. We also have Beatbot’s entire lineup of robot pool cleaners at new low prices starting from $284, as well as Segway e-scooters with increased savings, all the Prime Day EGO Power+ tool deals, Navee EVs, and much more waiting for you below.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: China’s EV exports smash another record as Southeast Asia goes electric
summary: China’s EV export machine shows no signs of slowing down, and demand from Southeast Asia is a big part of it.

title: Ford believes it has an edge over China with its new affordable EV platform
summary: Ford’s new Universal Electric Vehicle (UEV) platform is set to underpin a series of low-cost EVs, but can it compete with China?

title: Toyota scrapped the flagship Lexus EV, but a successor just got the green light
summary: Toyota will not move forward with the Lexus LF-ZC. While the flagship EV has been canceled, the next-gen technology that it was set to use, including new batteries, a dedicated platform, and gigacasting, is ready. A company executive confirmed that a successor is in the works.

title: Prime Day 2026 Green Deals: EVs, power stations, tools, smart devices, and much more
summary: Amazon’s Prime Day Sale event is officially live, and with it we’re getting a ton of amazing Green Deals and beyond – both on the marketplace website and from direct brand websites. The savings train has pulled into the station and will stick around for four days, giving folks some of the best prices on our favorite eco-friendly tech. Below, we’ve gathered up all the best deals on EVs, power stations, electric tools, and so much more, all in one place for all your one-stop shopping needs. We will be regularly updating this hub over the course of the event, so check back later if nothing catches your eye yet.

title: Tesla Is Giving Away Free Supercharging. Here's How To Win.
summary: Tesla is gamifying charging and will reward the winners with free Supercharging for the life of the car. Here's how to win.

title: The Peaq Is Peak Skoda. Too Bad America Can’t Buy It
summary: The Peaq is a big, sensible, seven-seat electric SUV with serious space and range and a very reasonable price.

title: Slate's $24,950 EV Truck Will Test What America Really Wants
summary: The Slate will be both the country’s cheapest new electric vehicle and its lowest-priced pickup. But will it be an automatic slam dunk?

title: Rivian Discontinues Its Cheapest R1T And R1S As R2 Rolls Out
summary: Rivian flagged earlier this year that the Dual Standard variants of its large SUV and truck were on the way out.

title: This Ex-Rental Tesla's Battery Was Heavily Degraded. Then The Decline Slowed
summary: After losing 20% of its capacity in its first two years, this abused Tesla’s battery degradation appears to have almost plateaued.

title: Rivian Has A Bold Plan For You To Skip The Repair Shop
summary: Rivian says 80% of EV repairs could eventually be handled by its mobile fleet, reducing the need for visits to physical service centers.

title: A Driverless Xiaomi Electric SUV Just Lapped The Ring. We Have Questions
summary: A 10-minute Nurburgring lap time isn't impressive. The fact that there is nobody in the driver’s seat is what makes this unique.

title: Tesla FSD Finally Reached Europe. Now Speed Limits May Slow Its Expansion
summary: Sweden has a problem with FSD breaking the speed limit on demand.

title: A Tesla Model Y Battery Test Looked Bad. Then The Degradation Almost Stopped
summary: After a sharp early drop, this Tesla Model Y's recent battery health test suggests this mostly fast-charged LFP pack has settled down.

title: License Plate Cameras Are About To Start Tracking A Lot More Than Just Your Car
summary: These next-gen ALPR systems look to bridge the gap between you and your car—which means tracking your airpods, wearables, and more.
